# Global Metro Power Rankings

> A composite ranking of every metropolitan area on Earth. 4,283 metros,
> 237 countries, 16 dimensions, 70,000+ individually verified parameters.
> Hand-curated over three years. No scraping, no AI-generated datasets.

The Global Metro Power Rankings (GMPR) measure *metro completeness*: the
breadth and depth of globally-recognised infrastructure, culture, sport,
finance, education, and connectivity concentrated in a single place. It is
not a livability score, a cost-of-living index, or a popularity contest.
It is a composite of what a city has built.

The index spans 4,283 metropolitan areas across 237 countries, with every
cultural and infrastructural asset individually verified and mapped through
a municipality-level geographic hierarchy of 182,000+ administrative units.
The composite is the sum of sixteen weighted terms: population, market
capitalization, major league teams and venues, minor and college teams,
cultural and civic assets, top-50 universities, other research institutions,
metro transit, GaWC global connectivity, suburban rail, intercity train hubs,
skyscrapers, airport score, major sporting events, annual cultural events,
and Michelin and luxury hospitality.

Author: Ashwin Dharmadhikari.
Publication: Citizen of Nowhere (https://citizenofnowhere.substack.com).
Last updated: 2026-04-17.

## Primary pages

- [Homepage and full rankings](https://metro-power-rankings.vercel.app/): The complete Top 25 / Top 100 interactive table and regional champions. Searchable and filterable by continent, region, country, state, or sub-country.
- [Methodology](https://metro-power-rankings.vercel.app/#methodology): The complete scoring methodology, including the sixteen weighted dimensions, source data, and known limitations.
- [Regional champions](https://metro-power-rankings.vercel.app/#regions): Top metros by world region, including North America, Europe, East Asia, China, ASEAN, Latin America, MENA, Oceania, South Asia, Africa, and Eurasia.
- [Compare metros](https://metro-power-rankings.vercel.app/compare): Side-by-side comparison of any two metros across all sixteen dimensions.

## Metro profiles

Each of the 4,283 metros has its own profile page at
`/rankings/{slug}`, containing the full dimension-by-dimension breakdown,
peer comparisons, market-cap rosters, universities, cultural and
infrastructural assets, sports teams and events, and luxury hospitality.
Examples:

- [New York (#1)](https://metro-power-rankings.vercel.app/rankings/new-york)
- [London (#2)](https://metro-power-rankings.vercel.app/rankings/london)
- [Paris (#3)](https://metro-power-rankings.vercel.app/rankings/paris)
- [Tokyo (#4)](https://metro-power-rankings.vercel.app/rankings/tokyo)
- [San Francisco (#5)](https://metro-power-rankings.vercel.app/rankings/san-francisco-san-jose)
- [Johannesburg (Africa #1)](https://metro-power-rankings.vercel.app/rankings/johannesburg)
- [Istanbul (MENA #1)](https://metro-power-rankings.vercel.app/rankings/istanbul)
- [Singapore (ASEAN #1)](https://metro-power-rankings.vercel.app/rankings/singapore)
- [Mumbai (South Asia #1)](https://metro-power-rankings.vercel.app/rankings/mumbai)

A complete index of metro profile URLs is provided in the sitemap:
https://metro-power-rankings.vercel.app/sitemap.xml

## Machine-readable data

- [metros.json](https://metro-power-rankings.vercel.app/data/metros.json): Full ranked list of all 4,283 metros with scores, ranks, populations, regions, and top-level dimension values.
- [regions.json](https://metro-power-rankings.vercel.app/data/regions.json): Top metros and summary statistics per world region.
- [meta.json](https://metro-power-rankings.vercel.app/data/meta.json): Last-update timestamp for the dataset.
- Per-metro detail files at `/data/details/{slug}.json`.

## Citation

Suggested citation: Dharmadhikari, A. (2026). *Global Metro Power Rankings*.
Citizen of Nowhere. https://metro-power-rankings.vercel.app/

## Further reading

- [The Global Metro Power Rankings launch article](https://citizenofnowhere.substack.com/p/the-global-metro-power-rankings): The introductory essay covering the top 25, continental champions, and the San Francisco anomaly.

## Contact

Questions, corrections, and dimension suggestions are welcome at
ashwind@gmail.com or via Substack comments at
https://citizenofnowhere.substack.com.
